rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GERTIE VICTORIA FOSTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. A proper "response" to an order to appear or show cause for failing to close an estate timely is to FILE A PETITION either for final distribution or to report on the status of administration and request additional time for estate administration. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. PLEASE HAVE YOUR CASE NUMBER AND NAME READY FOR THE CLERK.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FELIPE R. VELA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition By The Ibarra Professional Law Corporation To Compel The Administrator To Pay The Statutory Fees Pursuant To The Order For Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/20/2023 (no earlier date available): 1) No proposed order has been received. A courtesy copy of the petition must also be provided. 2) The supplement filed on 01/11/2023 must be treated as an amendment requiring new notice (which must be given on the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120 with a copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6)), as the supplement requested payment of the $435 filing fee as additional relief sought. 3) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) actually served with the DE-120 form must be attached to the proof of service. 4) An allegation must be made as to whether the estate currently has sufficient funds advanced by the personal representative to pay the petitioner's fees and costs approved by the order for final distribution. If the estate has no such funds, a memorandum of points and authorities must be filed to explain why the personal representative should be held personally liable. See Zagoren v. Superior Court (1931) 117 Cal. App. 548, 552. 5) If the petitioner is seeking an order compelling the personal representative to sell or encumber the real property (or an order imposing a lien on the subject real property interest to secure the petitioner's fees and costs), a memorandum of points and authorities must be filed to explain whether the Court has authority over the real property interest that had already been distributed to the beneficiary (who is the personal representative) and is no longer an asset of the estate. The petitioner has not appealed or moved to set aside the order for final distribution to restore the Court's jurisdiction over the real property interest that had been distributed. See Estate of Baird (1919) 181 Cal. 742, 744; see also Estate of Lee (1981) 124 Cal. App. 3d 687, 692-693. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHELE PAYNE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition By Administrator To Establish Estates Claim Of Ownership To Royalty Interests And For Order Directing Its Transfer To Estate And Heirs
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/27/2023 (no earlier date available): 1) No proposed order has been received. A courtesy copy of the petition must also be provided. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-115 form. 3) The proof of service does not show the decedent's surviving spouse (James Ostroff) was served with copies of the DE-115 form and petition. 4) Crosspointe 2, LLC must be served at least 30 days before the hearing in the manner required for a summons in a civil action under Probate Code section 851(a)(2). 5) Item 3 of the DE-115 form must provide a sufficient description of the subject property ("LLC" is not the subject property). 6) The Nevada counsel's declaration attached to the petition must include language required by CCP section 2015.5(b) ("under the laws of the State of California" unless place of execution is within California). 7) A proof must be filed to show AJ Salomon's 50% interest in the LLC passed to the decedent (spousal property order, order for final distribution, assignment of interest, etc.). If there is no such proof, the personal representative of AJ Salomon's estate must be identified, or his heirs (if intestate) or devisees (if testate) must be identified if no probate administration is pending or anticipated. 8) A copy of the certificate of dissolution of the LLC filed with the Nevada Secretary of State must be filed. If the LLC's charter has been revoked, see Nevada Revised Statutes ("NRS") 86.4895; see also NRS 86.531-2(c)(certificate of dissolution must be signed by the personal representative of the last remaining member, if there is no remaining manager or member, unless otherwise provided in the articles of organization or operating agreement). 9) NRS 86.505-1 provides that a dissolved company continues as a company for the purpose of prosecuting claims of any kind or nature by it and of enabling it gradually to settle and close its business, to collect and discharge its obligations, to dispose of and convey its property, and to distribute its assets. NRS 86.541-2 provides that, if here are no managers or members, the personal representatives are, after filing the dissolution certificate, trustees of the dissolved company, with full power to prosecute claims of any kind or character by the company, to enable the company gradually to settle and close its business, to collect its assets, to collect and discharge its obligations, to dispose of and convey its property, to distribute its money and other property among the members, after paying or adequately providing for the payment of its liabilities and obligations, and to do every other act to wind up and liquidate its business and affairs. A memorandum of points and authorities must be filed to explain why the petitioner, as personal representative of the estate of the decedent as the last remaining member of the LLC and as trustee of the dissolved LLC, should not be required to follow the distribution priority and procedures set forth in NRS 86.521. 10) If Crosspointe 2, LLC is not recognizing the petitioner's authority as trustee of the dissolved LLC to receive royalty payments and is insisting on a court order to release the payments, a memorandum of points and authorities must be filed to explain why the petitioner should not be required to initiate a judicial dissolution proceeding in the Nevada district court under NRS 86.495 to obtain the order acceptable to Crosspointe 2, LLC. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ANGELO MARTINEZ SENDEJAS LVING TRUST DATED SEPTEMBER 29, 2004
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/15/2023. Please provide the following information seven days prior to the hearing: (1) Notice of Hearing [DE-115] and a copy of petition must be given to all heirs of the settlor, who are not the beneficiaries under the terms of the Trust, in the manner referred in PC 17203(b) as PC 851 (b). (2) Clarification if the Trust has no separate schedule listing the trust property (ex. Schedule A) (3) Clarification if Lauren and Lawrence are the identical beneficiary. If not, either Notice of Hearing with a copy of petition to be given to Lawrence who is a specific gift beneficiary. If the gift has been met, thus notice is not required, Petition must allege accordingly with a proof of satisfaction.

[LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] It appears that a guardian ad litem may need to be appointed to represent the minors' interests. (How old are the minors?) Notice must include the language for remote appearance procedures, as required by LRSF 14.1.D.6. Deadline to get the proposed order and courtesy copies of any e-filed papers to the assigned examiner is 3/17/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/7/2023. Please provide the original will or an authenticated copy of the will along with an authenticated copy of the order admitting the will to probate in the State of New Mexico. PC 12521. Also, proposed Order for Probate must be submitted. LRSF 14.7.A.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: DY DINH TRAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF DEATH
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.
